name: David's Song, Twice
notes: David's song of deliverance, preserved both in the history (2 Samuel 22) and in the Psalter (Psalm 18). Psalm 18 folds the narrative introduction into its superscription and merges 2 Samuel's opening rock-and-fortress couplet into a single verse, so from the call upon the LORD onward 2 Samuel runs one verse ahead of the Psalm. Two of 2 Samuel's verses (22:2 and 22:3) both map to Psalm 18:2. Wording differs in many small details throughout, but every line has a counterpart.
tension: The two copies differ in wording at many points (2 Samuel 22 splits the rock-and-fortress imagery that Psalm 18 merges), reflecting separate transmission.

links.label: the words of this song in the day that the LORD
The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer
the horn of my salvation
who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies
the floods of ungodly men made me afraid
the snares of death prevented me
In my distress I called upon the LORD
Then the earth shook and trembled
There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it
and darkness was under his feet
And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly
coals of fire
thundered
and scattered them
the foundations of the world were discovered
he drew me out of many waters
He delivered me from my strong enemy
They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay
he delivered me, because he delighted in me
according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me
For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God
For all his judgments were before me
I was also upright before him
according to my righteousness
With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful
With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself
by my God have I leaped over a wall
the word of the LORD is tried: he is a buckler to all
who is a rock
maketh my way perfect
He maketh my feet like hinds
He teacheth my hands to war
thy gentleness hath made me great
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me
I have pursued mine enemies
they are fallen under my feet
thou hast girded me with strength
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies
even unto the LORD, but he answered them not
Then did I beat them
delivered me from the strivings of
shall submit themselves unto me
afraid out of their close places
The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock
It is God that avengeth me
thou hast delivered me from the violent man
give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen
and to his seed for evermore
